content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

August 5, 1991

Bill Leonard
State Senator
STATE CAPITOL
P.O. Box 942848
Sacramento, CA  94248-0001

Re:  Your Request for Informal Assistance
     Our File No. I-91-369

Dear Senator Leonard:

     By letter dated July 15, 1991, you are seeking advice with
respect to your duties and obligations under the conflict of
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act ("the Act")¹ if
your spouse were employed by a firm registered to lobby the
California Legislature.

     Although your letter states that a "formal written opinion"
is requested, we understand your request to be more appropriately
one for written advice pursuant to Section 83114(b).  This
information was conveyed by telephone on August 5, 1991, to Ms.
Gina Rutherford of your staff.

     We are providing you with informal advice because your
inquiry does not reference a specific pending governmental
decision.²

     The Act precludes you from making, participating in the
making, or in any way using your official position to influence a
governmental decision in which you know or have reason to know you
are financially interested.  (Section 87100.)  Two sections of the
Act are pertinent to your inquiry.


_____________________________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.  Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations section
18000, et seq; all references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c).)

428 J Street, Suite 800  •  P.O. Box 807  •  Sacramento CA 95804-0807  •  (916) 322-5660

Our File No. I-91-369
Page 2


     Section 87103 ("Financial Interest") provides that you are
financially interested in a decision if it is reasonably
foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial
effect, distinguishable from the effect on the public generally,
on any source of income aggregating fifty dollars or
more in the twelve months prior to the time when the decision is
made. (Section 87103(c).) You would have a community property
interest in the income your spouse receives as an employee of a
lobbying firm. (Thornton Advice Letter, I-91-108; Morris Advice
Letter, I-90-373; Feerick Advice Letter, I-90-366.)

     Among other things, Section 87102.5 ("Legislature; Use of
Position to Influence Decisions") may prohibit your participation
in decisions in which you are financially interested in at least
two ways. First, your participation is precluded when you have
received income from a lobbyist employer who will be directly and
significantly impacted by the decision in a manner distinguishable
from the general public. (Section 87102.5(a)(6).) And second,
your participation is prohibited when the decision will directly
and significantly financially impact an income source to you when
the income is derived from lobbying activities. (Section
87102.5(a)(7).) Again, you would have a community property
interest in your spouse's income.

     Obviously, more specific advice is contingent upon facts
particular to both the decision being made and the relationship
between the decision and a lobbyist employer or lobbying firm.
There may be decisions for which your participation would not be
precluded because the requisite relationships between the decision
and your (community property) source of income - through your
spouse's employment - would not be present.

     A more detailed letter outlining the guidelines applicable to
assist you in the determination whether you have a disqualifying
conflict of interest will be forthcoming in the near future. In
the interim, please contact me at (916) 322-5901 if you have any
further questions regarding this matter or would like to
supplement your inquiry with additional information concerning a
pending decision.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Acting General Counsel

                                        By: Jonathan S. Rothman
                                            Counsel, Legal Division

SH:JSR:dg
